description: Letter/petition from a woman, in Bilbays, to Abū ʿImrān b. Muwaffaq, in Fustat, the square of the moneychangers. In Judaeo-Arabic. Dating: probably 12th or 13th century. She asks the addressee to speak to her brother (probably Sālim al-Naqqād al-Ṣayrafī) and rebuke him for not helping her in this foreign land. Her travels have not been by her own choice; she was looking for fortune and has attained the opposite; now she is 'perplexed and cut-off.' Later she specifies that she only left to reach al-Shām (Damascus?) but never made it. If they want her to come to Fustat, they must send her the costs of the journey. (Information in part from Mediterranean Society, I, p. 448)
description_he: מכתב/עתירה מאשה, בבלביס, לאבו עמראן ב' מופק, בפוסטאט. ביהודית-ערבית. כנראה מאה 12 או 13. מבקשת ממנו לדבר עם אחיה ולנזוף בו על כך שאינו עוזר לה בארץ הזרה. אינה יכולה לשוב ללא כספי הדרך.
